summary.low_structured:
• React component for stock chart
• Uses Recharts for visualization
• Displays company and ticker info
summary.high_unstructured: This code implements a React component called StockChart that visualizes stock closing prices over time using the Recharts library. It first checks if the provided data prop is valid, then extracts the company name and ticker symbol for display. The component processes the input data to create an array of objects with date and price fields, suitable for the LineChart. The chart is rendered responsively, includes axes, a tooltip, a legend, and a monotone line representing the stock's closing price, and is styled with a margin at the top and a specific line color.
summary.medium_structured:
• Receives stock data as a prop and checks for validity
• Extracts company name and ticker for display
• Formats data for use in a Recharts LineChart
• Renders a responsive chart with axes, tooltip, legend, and a price line
summary.medium_unstructured: The StockChart component takes stock data as a prop and renders a line chart of closing prices over time using the Recharts library. It displays the company name and ticker, and only renders if valid data is provided. The chart includes axes, a tooltip, a legend, and a styled line for the price.
summary.low_unstructured: This code defines a React component that displays a stock price line chart using Recharts.
summary.high_structured:
• Defines a StockChart React component for visualizing stock data
• Validates the input data prop before rendering
  ◦ Ensures data, data.data, and data.data.length are present
• Extracts and displays the company name and ticker symbol
• Processes input data to create chartData array
  ◦ Each entry contains a date and corresponding closing price
• Utilizes Recharts components to build the chart
  ◦ ResponsiveContainer ensures the chart scales with its container
  ◦ LineChart is configured with margins and uses chartData
  ◦ XAxis and YAxis display date and price axes, with Y-axis set to auto domain
  ◦ Tooltip and Legend provide interactivity and context
  ◦ Line component draws a monotone line for closing prices, styled with a specific color and no dots
• The chart is wrapped in a div with a top margin for layout spacing

summary.high_unstructured: The StockChart component is a React functional component that visualizes stock price data as a line chart. It first checks if the incoming data prop is valid and non-empty, returning null otherwise. The component extracts the company name and ticker symbol for display in the chart title, and transforms the raw data into a format suitable for Recharts, mapping each entry to an object with date and price fields. It then renders a responsive LineChart with labeled axes, a tooltip, a legend, and a styled line representing the stock's closing price over time.

summary.medium_unstructured: The StockChart component takes stock data as a prop and renders a line chart showing price over time. • Implements a React functional component named StockChart
• Handles input validation:
  ◦ Checks if the data prop and its data array are present and non-empty
  ◦ Returns null if validation fails
• Extracts and displays company name and ticker symbol in the chart header
• Defines a helper function to format date strings (though not used in rendering)
• Transforms the input data into an array of objects with date and price keys for charting
• Uses Recharts components to render the chart:
  ◦ ResponsiveContainer ensures the chart scales with its container
  ◦ LineChart is configured with margins and the formatted data
  ◦ XAxis and YAxis display date and price axes, with auto-scaling for Y
  ◦ Tooltip and Legend are included for interactivity and clarity
  ◦ Line plots the price data as a smooth, monotone line without dots
• Applies a top margin to the chart container for spacing

summary.high_unstructured: This Express route handles GET requests to '/api/stock/:symbol' by extracting the symbol parameter and converting it to uppercase. If the USE_MOCK flag is true, it generates and returns mock stock data for the symbol. Otherwise, it fetches historical daily stock data starting from January 1, 2024, using the Yahoo Finance API, extracts relevant fields (date, close, volume), and constructs a response object including the ticker, company name, and the data array. The response is sent as JSON, and any errors encountered during processing are logged and result in a 500 error response.

  ◦ Mock data is generated and returned if enabled
  ◦ Otherwise, fetches daily stock quotes from Yahoo Finance
• Responds with JSON containing ticker, name, and daily data
• Handles errors by logging and returning a 500 status

  ◦ If true, calls generateMockData to create and return mock stock data
  ◦ If false, fetches historical daily stock data from Yahoo Finance starting from 2024-01-01
    ◦ Extracts quotes and company name from the API response
    ◦ Maps quote data to include only date, close, and volume fields
• Constructs a response object with ticker, name, and data array

• Uses Recharts for rendering the line chart

summary.high_unstructured: This code implements a StockChart React component that visualizes stock closing prices over time using the Recharts library. It first checks for valid input data, extracts the company name and ticker, and formats the raw data into an array of objects with date and price fields. The component includes a custom date formatter for the x-axis, displaying only the month abbreviation, and renders a responsive line chart with axes, tooltip, legend, and a styled line representing the price. The chart is wrapped in a container with a margin and displays the company name and ticker as a heading above the chart.
summary.medium_structured:
• Defines a React component that visualizes stock data as a line chart
• Accepts a data prop containing company info and price data
• Formats dates for the x-axis to display month abbreviations
• Renders chart elements: axes, tooltip, legend, and line
  ◦ Displays company name and ticker above the chart
summary.medium_unstructured: The StockChart component renders a line chart of stock prices using the Recharts library. It takes a data prop, formats the data for the chart, and displays the company name and ticker. The chart includes axes, a tooltip, a legend, and a formatted month label for the x-axis.
summary.low_unstructured: This code defines a React component that displays a stock price line chart using Recharts.
summary.high_structured:
• Implements a React functional component named StockChart
• Validates the input data prop, returning null if data is missing or empty
• Extracts company name and ticker symbol from the data
• Processes raw stock data:
  ◦ Maps each data point to an object with 'date' and 'price' fields for Recharts
• Defines a date formatting function for the x-axis:
  ◦ Converts date strings to month abbreviations (e.g., 'Jan', 'Feb')
• Renders a styled container with a heading showing company and ticker
• Uses ResponsiveContainer and LineChart from Recharts to display:
  ◦ XAxis with custom tick formatting and styling
  ◦ YAxis with automatic domain
  ◦ Tooltip and Legend for interactivity
  ◦ Line representing stock price, styled and without dots
